title: BUG: Add delimiters to NameObject.renumber_table
body:
Per the spec, name objects cannot contain unescaped delimiters, which include angle, curly and square brackets (currently missing).

I've attached a sample input PDF with a valid, correctly escaped name object. When appending this to a new PdfWriter the output is invalid (incorrectly escaped):

```
>>> w = PdfWriter()
>>> w.append('nameobj.pdf')
>>> w.write('out.pdf')
```
[out.pdf](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/20173760/out.pdf)
[nameobj.pdf](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/20173761/nameobj.pdf)

step_plan.summary: Add missing hex-escape mappings for angle, square and curly bracket delimiters to NameObject.renumber_table so NameObject.renumber() correctly escapes them when writing PDFs; include a regression test to verify the expected #XX escapes.

steps.operation: Add missing delimiter mappings to NameObject.renumber_table (and include a regression test to assert correct escaping)
steps.reason: NameObject.renumber() relies on renumber_table to escape characters when producing bytes for writing PDFs. The PDF spec requires delimiters (<, >, [, ], {, }) to be escaped in name objects. These delimiters are recognized when reading (present in delimiter_pattern) but are missing from renumber_table, causing unescaped delimiters in output. Adding explicit mappings ('<': b'#3C', '>': b'#3E', '[': b'#5B', ']': b'#5D', '{': b'#7B', '}': b'#7D') will ensure renumber() emits the correct #XX escapes using the same two-digit uppercase hex style as existing entries.
steps.side_effects: Primary effect: written PDFs will escape the six delimiter characters in name objects, preventing invalid output; this is a backward-compatible encoding change for name serialization. Tests: add or update a regression test (e.g., read the provided sample nameobj.pdf or construct a NameObject containing these delimiters, then write and assert the name bytes contain the expected #3C/#3E/#5B/#5D/#7B/#7D escapes). Dependents: any code that relied on prior (incorrect) behavior should not break because this enforces spec-compliant escaping; downstream consumers of PdfWriter output will see properly escaped names. API: no public API change. Potentially, golden-file or snapshot tests that assert exact serialized output may need updating if they previously included unescaped delimiters (they were invalid per spec and should be corrected).
